seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Workflow Automation with Claude Tool Use enables powerful multi-step agents that integrate APIs, handle conditional logic, and incorporate human approvals for complex task orchestration. However, native tool chains are prone to cascading failures, conflicting parallel results, and inconsistent reasoning that can derail workflows and cause costly errors. Vex guardrails wrap Claude's tool_use responses to verify action correctness, permissions, and idempotency, ensuring reliable automation at scale.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a Claude agent that receives tasks, reasons through multi-step execution using parallel tool calls, integrates with external APIs (e.g., CRM, payment systems), applies conditional branching based on results, and pauses for human-in-the-loop approvals on high-risk actions. Tool definitions include API wrappers for idempotent operations, rollback mechanisms, and permission checks. The agent maintains conversation state across steps, with Vex verification layered on each tool_use response to catch errors before execution.

Key components include a task queue, Claude's messages.create() API for agent reasoning, parallel tool execution for efficiency, and a state machine for workflow progression. Human approval gates use separate tool calls that block until confirmed, while rollback tools ensure safe failure recovery. This setup handles medium-risk workflows like order processing or customer onboarding.

Vex integrates at the tool_use boundary, verifying outputs against specs for action correctness, permission compliance, idempotency guarantees, and rollback availability before permitting execution.

risks.name: Cascading Tool Failures
risks.description: Early API tool failures propagate through workflow, causing inconsistent state and unrecoverable errors
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies each tool response before chain continuation, triggering rollback on first failure

risks.name: Parallel Tool Conflicts
risks.description: Concurrent API calls return conflicting data (e.g., inventory vs payment race conditions)
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces sequential verification and conflict resolution rules on parallel results

risks.name: Inconsistent Reasoning
risks.description: Agent's extended thinking contradicts final tool choice or parameters
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-validates reasoning traces against tool_use parameters and workflow specs

risks.name: Unauthorized Actions
risks.description: Agent calls high-risk tools without proper permission checks or human approval
risks.mitigation: Vex permission validation blocks execution until human-in-loop approval confirmed

implementation.title: Parallel Tool Calls
implementation.description: Execute inventory and pricing checks concurrently for efficiency
implementation.code:
import anthropic

# After first tool response with parallel_calls
response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=tools,
    messages=[last_message]
)

# Handle parallel results
for tool_result in response.content:
    if tool_result.type == "tool_use":
        print(f"Tool {tool_result.name}: {tool_result.input}")

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Verification Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Wrap messages.create() tool_use responses with vex.verify() for action correctness
vexIntegration.code:
import anthropic
from vex_sdk import verify

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=tools,
    messages=messages
)

# Vex verification for each tool use
for content in response.content:
    if content.type == "tool_use":
        verified = verify(
            spec={
                "action_correctness": True,
                "permissions": ["payment_process"],
                "idempotent": True,
                "rollback_available": True
            },
            observation=content,
            risk_profile="medium"
        )
        if not verified:
            await rollback_workflow(content.input.order_id)
            break
